docs.title: Insurance AI Learning Library
docs.text:
這是一個可分享的 web-style learning library，用於保險 POS、Sales Journey AI、BRD reformatting 和 AI use case prioritisation 的學習與討論。

Disclaimer

本網站僅作個人學習與討論用途，並非客戶建議、法律意見、合規意見或正式項目建議。

docs.title: How To Use This Site
docs.text:
• 如果要快速建立背景，先讀 Client POS / Sales Platform Preparation 和 Insurance POS Basics。
  • 如果要準備 AI 討論，讀 Sales Journey AI 和 AI Use Case Prioritisation。
  • 如果要準備 team / project lead / client 對話，直接看 Meeting / Discussion Outputs。

docs.title: Recommended First Read
docs.text:
1. Start Here
  2. Learning Index
  3. Insurance POS Basics
  4. Product Configuration
  5. Sales Journey AI Opportunities
  6. Talking Points

docs.title: Working Principle
docs.text:
本 library 用 AI Transformation Pathfinder 角度整理材料：用 BA、PM、FDE 三個 lens，把業務需求、交付節奏、系統可行性、資料限制和 AI governance 連起來。

討論 AI 時，應連到 user pain point、approved data source、human review、audit trail 同 compliance sign-off。

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text: 呢頁幫讀者按當前目標快速決定讀咩。唔需要由頭讀到尾，按情境揀材料就得。

docs.title: If I only have 10 minutes
docs.text:
Recommended pages

  • Learning Index
  • Talking Points

Focus

  • 先掌握已知事實、工作假設、待確認問題。
  • 記住 BA + PM + FDE lens。

Practice

「我想先分清楚已知 facts、working assumptions 同 open questions，避免太早落 recommendation。」

docs.title: If I need to prepare for a project alignment discussion
docs.text:
Recommended pages

  • Project Alignment Questions
  • Client POS / Sales Platform Preparation

Focus

  • 問清楚 priority、expected output、stakeholders、next milestone。
  • 不要只問「我要做咩」，要問「咩 output 對 team 最有用」。

Practice

「下次 touchpoint 前，你覺得交 question list、current-state summary、AI use case view，定 BRD tracker draft 最有幫助？」

docs.title: If I need to understand insurance POS / sales platform
docs.text:
Recommended pages

  • Insurance POS Basics
  • Product Configuration

Focus

  • 搞清楚 sales journey：FNA、illustration、application、document upload、submission。
  • 留意 product rules 點樣影響 quote、NIGO、referral、POS configuration。

Practice

「今次 POS scope 係咪 cover quote、illustration、application、document upload 同 submission，定只係其中一部分？」

docs.title: If I need to discuss Sales Journey AI
docs.text:
Recommended pages

  • AI Opportunities in Insurance
  • Sales Journey AI Talking Points
  • Identification Playbook

Focus

  • 先講 lower-risk, agent-facing AI。
  • Product recommendation、suitability、underwriting decisioning 唔好當 quick wins。

Practice

「我建議先由 approved-source、agent-facing、human-reviewed use cases 開始，例如 knowledge assistant、document checklist 同 pre-submission quality check。」

docs.title: If I need to prepare BRD / template reformat work
docs.text:
Recommended pages

  • BRD Reformat Playbook
  • Product Configuration

Focus

  • 先確認 a batch of legacy templates/items 係咩：products、templates、documents、rules，定 export rows。
  • 分清楚 documentation clean-up 同 system configuration / export mapping。

Practice

「Before reformatting，想先 confirm count unit、target export format、required fields 同 review owner。」

docs.title: If I need meeting-ready talking points
docs.text:
Recommended pages

  • Talking Points
  • Discovery Questions
  • Sales Journey AI Talking Points

Focus

  • 用香港 team 自然講法，保留必要英文專業詞。
  • 每句都盡量連到 scope、pain point、evidence、owner、dependency 或 control。

Practice

「為咗令 discussion actionable，我建議每個 pain point 都 capture journey step、owner、evidence、system touchpoint、control impact 同 next action。」

docs.title: AI Use Case Prioritisation
docs.text: 這一節幫讀者把 AI ideas 轉成可討論、可排序、可治理的 consulting output。

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• 用 Prioritisation Playbook 定義排序方法。
  • 用 Use Case Matrix 快速比較 user、pain point、data、control、metric 和 dependency。

docs.title: AI Use Case Prioritisation Playbook
docs.text: 這頁幫讀者把 AI ideas 排成可交付的 shortlist，避免只停留在概念層。

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: 幫讀者把 Client POS / Sales Platform sales journey AI ideas 排優先次序，確保每個 use case 都有清楚 pain point、data、control、metric 和 delivery dependency。

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text:
• Team 開始討論「AI 可以放在哪裡」。
  • 需要把 AI ideas 變成 shortlist。
  • 需要準備 use case matrix、roadmap 或 pilot recommendation。

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. 從 pain point 開始
  2. 例如 NIGO、文件錯漏、quote 難解釋、產品規則難找。
  3. 定義 user 和 journey step
  4. agent、sales support、operations、underwriter、product team。
  5. 確認 data source
  6. approved product guide、SOP、application data、document checklist、BRD。
  7. 定義 AI support
  8. search、summary、field extraction、quality check、plain-language explanation。
  9. 設計 control
  10. citations、human review、audit log、approved wording、exception handling。
  11. 定義 metric
  12. NIGO rate、cycle time、search time、manual rework、QA defects。
  13. 分桶
  14. quick start、controlled pilot、deep integration、defer。

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text:
• Which pain point is this use case solving?
  • What data would the AI use, and is it approved?
  • Who reviews the output?
  • Could it affect suitability, underwriting, pricing, or customer communication?
  • Does it require POS integration?
  • What metric would show improvement?

docs.title: Risk / Compliance Considerations
docs.text:
• Customer-facing language needs approval and version control.
  • Product recommendation support needs suitability guardrails.
  • Sensitive health/financial data needs access and retention controls.
  • AI output used in sales or underwriting workflow should be logged.
  • Automated decisioning should be treated as high-risk unless governance is explicit.

docs.title: Expected Outputs
docs.text:
• Prioritised AI use case shortlist.
  • Scoring rationale.
  • Data and integration assumptions.
  • Governance / compliance notes.
  • Recommended next step for each use case.

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: 「我建議 AI use case priority 唔好只睇 technology appeal，而係睇 pain point、value、data readiness、control requirement 同 POS dependency。」

docs.title: AI Use Case Matrix

docs.text: 這頁是整套 learning library 的準備總覽，幫讀者用 facts、assumptions、open questions 方式理解保險 POS / sales platform transformation 工作。

docs.title: 工作脈絡
docs.text: 類型 內容 已知事實 本 library 聚焦 insurance POS / sales platform、Sales Journey AI、Product BRD / template reformat。 工作假設 Current POS / sales platform 可能支援 quotation、illustration、application、document upload、submission。 工作假設 AI 應先定位為輔助 agent / operations / product team，而非直接取代保險決策。 待確認問題 Target insurance client 的 market、產品線、channel、vendor scope、legacy templates/items 定義、export 格式。

docs.title: 三個工作 Lens
docs.text: Lens 要留意 BA 流程、需求、業務規則、例外處理、控制點、資料欄位 PM scope、owner、timeline、dependency、decision gate、交付物 FDE data flow、system touchpoint、integration、prototype / automation feasibility

docs.title: POS Assessment Playbook
docs.text: 這頁是 current POS / sales platform current-state assessment 的實戰方法，幫讀者把流程、系統、資料、vendor、產品規則和 AI 改善機會放在同一張分析地圖上。

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: 用結構化方式評估 target insurance client current POS / sales platform 的 current state：流程、用戶、系統、資料、產品規則、vendor 依賴、控制點，以及 AI / automation 改善機會。

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text:
• 準備 current POS / sales platform discovery workshop。
  • 整理 current-state assessment approach。
  • 需要把 POS pain points 轉成 recommendations。

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. 確認 scope
  2. product line、channel、user group、system boundary、expected output。
  3. 畫 current-state journey
  4. needs analysis、illustration、application、document upload、submission、underwriting handoff。
  5. 收集 evidence
  6. process map、screenshots、NIGO reasons、case logs、sample BRD、user feedback。
  7. 找 pain point 和 root cause
  8. process issue、system limitation、data issue、vendor dependency、control gap。
  9. 評估 POS capability
  10. quote、validation、document checklist、case tracking、reporting、configuration。
  11. 整理 options
  12. optimize current process、enhance current POS / sales platform、add AI assistant、improve integration、vendor change、longer-term platform change。

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text: Topic Questions Scope Which products, channels, and user groups are in scope? Journey Which steps are inside current POS / sales platform and which are outside? Pain point Where do agents or operations teams spend the most time? Data Which data is structured, reused, or rekeyed? Product rules Which rules are configured, manual, or vendor-controlled? Vendor What is the change request model and release cycle? Metrics Do we have NIGO rate, STP rate, cycle time, drop-off, or rework volume?

docs.title: Risk / Compliance Considerations
docs.text:
• Suitability and disclosure steps must be traceable.
  • Customer, health, and financial data need access and retention controls.
  • AI outputs should not bypass underwriting, compliance, or adviser review.
  • Vendor limitations may affect roadmap feasibility and cost.
  • Pain points should be backed by evidence, not only anecdote.

docs.title: Expected Outputs
docs.text:
• Current-state journey map with system touchpoints.
  • Pain point / root cause summary.
  • POS capability gap table.
  • Vendor and dependency assessment.
  • AI opportunity list with governance notes.
  • Prioritised next-step recommendations.

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: 「為咗令 POS assessment actionable，我建議每個 issue 都記錄 journey step、user impact、root cause、evidence、dependency 同 possible recommendation。」

docs.title: Insurance POS Basics
docs.text: 這一節解釋保險 POS / sales platform 基礎，以及 product configuration 如何影響 quotation、application、validation、NIGO 和 BRD reformat。

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• POS 不熟時，先讀 POS Basics。
  • 聽到 product rules、configuration、BRD、export fields 時，讀 Product Configuration。

docs.title: Insurance POS Basics
docs.text: 這頁解釋保險 POS / sales platform 的基本概念，適合初次了解 POS 時先建立共同語言。

docs.title: 概念
docs.text:
保險 POS（Point of Sale）通常指銷售前台平台：幫 agent / adviser 把客戶對話變成 needs analysis、quote / illustration、application、文件提交和 case tracking。

在 target insurance client 脈絡下，已知他們使用「current POS / sales platform」，但 current POS / sales platform 的實際範圍仍要向 team / client 確認。

docs.title: 為什麼對保險轉型重要
docs.text: POS 是保險銷售旅程的入口。如果 POS 設計不好，會造成重複輸入、quote 解釋困難、文件錯漏、NIGO、manual referral、產品上架慢，最後影響銷售體驗和營運效率。

docs.title: 典型 Journey
docs.text: 步驟 例子 讀者要觀察 Needs analysis agent 收集保障、儲蓄、健康需求 是否有標準流程和 audit trail？ Product selection 選 base plan 和 riders 規則是系統判斷還是人工理解？ Illustration 產生 premium / benefits projection 客戶是否容易理解？ Application 填 proposal / e-application 資料是否重複輸入？ Document upload 上載 KYC、醫療、財務文件 是否提交前已檢查完整性？ Submission 送 underwriting / operations NIGO 和 referral 的原因是什麼？

docs.title: Key Terms / 關鍵詞
docs.text: 專業詞 中文 / 說明 FNA / needs analysis 客戶需求分析，支持產品建議和 suitability。 Illustration 保費、保障、現金價值或利益示例。 Rider 附加保障。 Suitability 產品是否適合客戶需要和情況。 NIGO Not in good order，申請資料不完整或有錯。 STP Straight-through processing，無需人工返工即可處理。 Referral 因規則、風險或例外而轉人工審批。

docs.title: BA / PM Relevance
docs.text: Lens 讀者要做什麼 BA 拆 journey、記錄欄位、規則、痛點、控制點。 PM 確認 scope、stakeholder、timeline、交付物和依賴。 FDE 看資料在哪裡產生、如何流向 downstream systems、哪些步驟可自動化。

docs.title: 讀者可以問
docs.text:
• Which steps are actually performed inside current POS / sales platform?
  • Which steps happen outside current POS / sales platform, such as email, Excel, CRM, or manual review?
  • What are the top reasons for NIGO or referral?
  • Which product rules are validated before submission?
  • What evidence do we have: screenshots, process map, case logs, user feedback?

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: 「我想 confirm 一下，今次 current POS / sales platform scope 係咪 cover quote、illustration、application capture、document upload 同 submission，定只係其中一部分？」

docs.title: Insurance Product Configuration Basics
docs.text: 這頁解釋 product configuration 如何把產品規則轉成系統行為，特別適合連接 current POS / sales platform assessment 和 BRD reformat 工作。

docs.title: 概念
docs.text:
Product configuration 是把產品設計和 business rules 轉成系統可執行的設定，例如 eligibility、premium mode、rider rules、document requirements、underwriting triggers。

對 current POS / sales platform assessment 和 BRD reformat 來說，核心問題是：

哪些產品規則只是寫在文件，哪些已配置在 current POS / sales platform 或 downstream system？

docs.title: 為什麼對保險轉型重要
docs.text: 產品規則如果不清晰，會令 quote 錯、application 被 block、case 變 NIGO、underwriting 多 manual referral，也會拖慢新產品上架和產品變更。

docs.title: 簡單例子
docs.text: 規則類型 例子 系統影響 Eligibility Applicant age must be 18-60 current POS / sales platform 要檢查 age basis 和錯誤提示。 Premium mode Monthly / annual payment available 影響 illustration、payment 和 export fields。 Rider rule Rider only available with selected base plans 影響 product selection 和 validation。 Underwriting trigger Medical review needed above threshold 影響 referral 和 document checklist。 Channel rule Product available to agency only 影響 user access 和 sales journey。

docs.title: Key Terms / 關鍵詞
docs.text: 專業詞 中文 / 說明 Base plan 主產品。 Rider 附加產品或附加保障。 Eligibility rule 誰可以買、何時可以買。 Validation rule 系統檢查輸入是否符合規則。 Referral rule 何時要轉人工審批。 Configuration owner 負責維護系統規則的人或團隊。

docs.title: BA / PM Relevance
docs.text: Lens 讀者要留意 BA 規則 wording、例外、source traceability、business owner。 PM product change timeline、vendor dependency、approval path、testing window。 FDE rule location、data structure、API / import format、test cases。

docs.title: 讀者可以問
docs.text:
• Which rules are configured in current POS / sales platform versus handled manually?
  • Who owns product rule changes: product, IT, vendor, underwriting, or compliance?
  • How are riders and eligibility rules tested before launch?
  • What happens if BRD wording conflicts with system behavior?
  • Which rules most often create NIGO, referral, or agent confusion?

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: 「BRD / template reformat 時，我想分清楚邊啲係 documentation clean-up，邊啲要變成 POS configuration、validation logic 或 export fields。」

docs.title: Meeting / Discussion Outputs
docs.text: 這一節是會前可直接使用的問題、句子和 discussion material。

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• 會前 5 分鐘：讀 Talking Points。
  • 準備 client / team discovery：讀 Discovery Questions。
  • 跟 project lead 對焦：讀 Project Alignment Questions。

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text: 這頁是按 stakeholder 整理的 discovery questions，語氣貼近香港 team / client 對話方式，同時保留必要英文專業詞。

docs.title: 先問清楚 Scope
docs.text:
• 「今次 target insurance client scope 係邊個 market、channel 同 product line？」
  • 「今次講嘅 current POS / sales platform 包括邊啲功能？係 quote、illustration、application、document upload、submission 都包，定只係其中一部分？」
  • 「呢個 assessment 最後要支持咩 decision？係 current-state diagnostic、vendor review、roadmap，定 implementation prep？」
  • 「我哋今次係 expected to assess、recommend、prototype，定 prepare implementation？」
  • 「之前提到嘅 a batch of legacy templates/items 係咩單位？係 products、templates、documents、rules，定 export rows？」
  • 「Export 係指 export 去 Excel、CSV、BRD template、system import file，定 consolidated tracker？」

docs.title: 問 Business / Product Owner
docs.text:
• 「邊啲 product rules 最常改？」
  • 「邊啲 rules 最容易有 ambiguity 或者要靠人手 interpret？」
  • 「Product rule change 通常邊個 approve？Product、compliance、underwriting，定 IT？」
  • 「而家 BRD 主要係 documentation 用，定會直接影響 POS configuration？」
  • 「如果 BRD wording 同 system behavior 唔一致，通常點處理？」

docs.title: 問 Sales / Channel
docs.text:
• 「Agent 喺 sales journey 邊一步最花時間？」
  • 「客戶通常喺邊啲位最容易唔明？Quote、illustration、benefits、exclusions，定 application requirements？」
  • 「current POS / sales platform 有冇邊啲位令 agent 要用 workaround，例如 Excel、email、manual note？」
  • 「如果可以改善一個 current POS / sales platform step，sales team 最想改邊個？」
  • 「有冇常見 case 係 agent 明明覺得應該 submit 到，但 system block 咗或者之後變 NIGO？」

docs.title: 問 Operations / Underwriting
docs.text:
• 「Top NIGO reasons 係咩？」
  • 「邊啲 supporting documents 最常 missing 或者 upload 錯？」
  • 「邊啲 validation 如果可以早啲做，就可以減少 rework？」
  • 「Underwriting handoff 時，通常缺少咩 case information？」
  • 「Referral case 最常見原因係 product rule、medical、financial、compliance，定資料唔完整？」

docs.title: 問 IT / Current POS / Sales Platform / Vendor
docs.text:
• 「邊啲 rules 可以喺 current POS / sales platform configure？邊啲要 vendor support？」
  • 「Product change / rule change 嘅 release cycle 通常幾耐？」
  • 「current POS / sales platform 同 CRM、underwriting、policy admin、payment、document system 有咩 integration？」
  • 「Journey data、case data、document status 可唔可以 extract 出嚟做 reporting 或 AI use case？」
  • 「而家有咩 audit log、role-based access、data retention controls？」
  • 「Vendor 有冇現成 AI feature、API、integration hook，定要另外 build？」

docs.title: 問 Compliance / Risk
docs.text:
• 「Sales journey 邊啲 step 一定要 suitability、disclosure、consent 或 approval evidence？」
  • 「邊類 AI use case 需要 compliance / legal / risk review？」
  • 「Customer、health、financial data 有咩使用限制？」
  • 「如果 AI support agent 或 operations work，要 log 啲咩？」
  • 「AI output 可唔可以直接俾 customer 睇？定一定要 agent review？」

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Close
docs.text: 「為咗之後 assessment 可以 actionable，我建議每個 pain point 都 capture：journey step、owner、evidence、system touchpoint、control impact 同 potential next action。」

docs.title: Project Alignment Questions
docs.text: 這頁幫項目成員同 project lead 對焦，確認下一步優先級、交付物和準備方向。

docs.title: Opening
docs.text: 「我想用 BA、PM、FDE 三個 lens 準備 insurance transformation workstream。想同你 confirm 一下，下一步 focus 喺邊度會最有用？」

docs.title: Scope / Priority
docs.text:
• 「下一步最應該產出咩？Question list、current-state summary、deck content、BRD tracker，定 meeting notes？」
  • 「而家 priority 係current POS / sales platform assessment、AI use cases、BRD reformat，定 product configuration？」
  • 「會主要 support 邊類 stakeholder？Business、IT、vendor、compliance、operations，定 sales？」
  • 「下次 internal / client meeting 前，最需要先補邊方面 context？」
  • 「有冇已知 hypothesis 或 leadership concern，需要先 keep in mind？」

docs.title: Current POS / Sales Platform
docs.text:
• 「今次最重要嘅 sales journey steps 係邊幾個？」
  • 「Team 係咪已經知道某啲 POS pain points？」
  • 「有冇現成 materials 可以睇，例如 screenshots、process maps、issue logs、BRDs、NIGO data？」
  • 「Product rules 主要係 configure 喺 current POS / sales platform、downstream systems，定而家好多都係 manual process？」
  • 「如果要快速補 POS 背景，有邊幾個 terms 或流程應該先搞清楚？」

docs.title: AI Transformation
docs.text:
• 「係要 propose new AI use cases，定 assess client 已有 ideas？」
  • 「AI scope 應該先 focus agent-facing、operations-facing、product-team-facing，定 customer-facing？」
  • 「有冇邊啲 AI topic 對 client / compliance 特別 sensitive，要避免講得太過？」
  • 「應該用邊個 governance lens 去睇？Privacy、suitability、auditability、model risk，定全部都要 cover？」
  • 「如果要 prioritise AI use cases，team 偏向睇 value、feasibility、risk、data readiness，定 integration dependency？」

docs.title: BRD / Template
docs.text:
• 「A batch of legacy templates/items 具體係咩？Products、riders、documents、rules，定 export rows？」
  • 「BRD task 係純 reformat documents，定要 extract product rules、create tracker、map export fields？」
  • 「Reformatted requirements 之後邊個 validate？」
  • 「需唔需要保留 source traceability，例如 original wording、file name、section/page？」
  • 「如果 old template 有 missing / ambiguous rules，應該點 log 同 assign owner？」

docs.title: Closing
docs.text: 「為咗確保時間用得準，下次 touchpoint 前你覺得交邊樣最有幫助：question list、current-state summary、AI use case view，定 BRD tracker draft？」

docs.title: Talking Points
docs.text: 這頁收集讀者可直接在香港 team / client 對話中使用的繁中 talking points，並保留必要英文專業詞。

docs.title: Positioning
docs.text: 我會用 AI Transformation Pathfinder 嘅角度準備：一方面理解 target insurance client current POS / sales platform 銷售流程同產品規則，另一方面留意 delivery feasibility、system dependency 同 responsible AI controls。

docs.title: Safe Project Framing
docs.text:
• 已知：target insurance client、current POS / sales platform、sales journey AI、BRD / template reformat 都係準備範圍。
  • 假設：current POS / sales platform 可能係銷售前台平台，但實際 scope 要同 team / client 確認。
  • 待問：a batch of legacy templates/items 係咩單位？export 去咩格式？AI 期望係 assessment、roadmap、prototype，定 implementation prep？

docs.title: 香港團隊可用講法
docs.text:
• 「我想先 confirm 一下，今次 POS scope 係咪主要 cover quote、illustration、application、document upload 同 submission？」
  • 「呢個 pain point 係偏 process 問題、system limitation、data quality、vendor dependency，定 control / compliance 問題？」
  • 「呢條 product rule 係已經 configure 咗喺 current POS / sales platform，定而家主要靠 manual handling？」
  • 「我哋有冇 evidence 支持呢個 pain point？例如 NIGO data、cycle time、user feedback、sample cases？」
  • 「呢個改善應該當 quick improvement、vendor enhancement，定 longer-term transformation item？」

docs.title: BA / PM / FDE 講法
docs.text:
• BA：「我想 capture 清楚 business rule、user impact、exception handling 同 control point。」
  • PM：「我哋可唔可以 confirm owner、dependency、decision date 同 expected output？」
  • FDE：「我想理解呢啲 data 喺邊度 create、點樣流去其他 system、同埋係咪 structured。」

docs.title: AI Governance 講法
docs.text:
• 「未講 automation 之前，我哋應該先定義 human review point。」
  • 「我哋要分清楚 AI-assisted recommendation 同 automated decision-making。」
  • 「如果會用到 customer、health 或 financial data，就要先確認 access、retention、consent 同 audit trail。」
  • 「Agent-facing answer 最好要 grounded 喺 approved product / process / compliance materials。」
  • 「每個 AI use case 都應該講得出 pain point、data source、control、metric 同 POS dependency。」

docs.title: Strong Close
docs.text: 為咗令 discussion actionable，我建議每個 opportunity 都用同一個格式記錄：journey step、pain point、data dependency、control requirement、metric 同 next decision。

docs.title: Product BRD / Template Reformat
docs.text: 這一節聚焦「old template → reformat」和「a batch of legacy templates/items → export」這條工作線，幫讀者分清楚 count unit、target format、source traceability 和 review owner。

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• 如果 team 提到 a batch of legacy templates/items，先用 playbook 確認 count unit。
  • 如果要準備 project alignment，可搭配 Project Alignment Questions。

docs.title: Product BRD Reformat Playbook
docs.text: 這頁是處理 legacy BRD / template reformat 的工作方法，特別適合釐清「a batch of legacy templates/items」和 export 需求。

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: 把舊 product BRD / template 轉成一致、可審閱、可追溯、可 export 的 requirement structure，支援 POS configuration、產品治理、migration 或後續 automation。

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text:
• Team 提到「old template → reformat」。
  • 需要處理「a batch of legacy templates/items」但未確認 item 單位。
  • 需要把產品文件變成 structured rules / tracker / export fields。

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. 確認 count unit
  2. product、rider、document、rule、section、export row。
  3. 建立 inventory
  4. ID、source file、owner、format、status、open questions。
  5. 抽取 facts
  6. 不先改寫意思，先保留 source wording 和位置。
  7. 標準化 requirement
  8. 用 must / should / may / exception / open question。
  9. 映射到 current POS / sales platform / downstream impact
  10. validation、screen field、document checklist、referral、export field。
  11. review 和 sign-off
  12. product owner、IT/current POS / sales platform owner、compliance 或 underwriting 視規則而定。

docs.title: Simple Example
docs.text: Old Wording Standard Requirement Export / System Impact Open Question Applicant should be 18-60 Applicant must be aged 18 to 60 inclusive at application date min_age, max_age, age_basis Applies to rider also? Monthly payment available Product must support monthly premium mode where eligible payment_mode=monthly Any minimum premium? Medical may be needed current POS / sales platform must flag medical referral based on defined underwriting rules referral trigger Rule stored in current POS / sales platform or underwriting system?

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text:
• What exactly are a batch of legacy templates/items?
  • What is the target output: BRD, Excel tracker, CSV, system import, or approval pack?
  • Who owns each product rule?
  • Which rules must be configured in current POS / sales platform?
  • Do we need source traceability for every reformatted requirement?
  • How should missing or ambiguous rules be handled?

docs.title: Risk / Compliance Considerations
docs.text:
• Do not silently reinterpret unclear product rules.
  • Keep source references for audit and reviewer confidence.
  • Suitability, disclosure, underwriting, and premium rules need careful review.
  • AI extraction can accelerate drafting, but human sign-off is required.
  • Export-ready does not mean business-approved unless approval is recorded.

docs.title: Expected Outputs
docs.text:
• BRD inventory tracker.
  • Standardized requirement template.
  • Open question log with owners.
  • Export field mapping.
  • Review / approval status.

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: 「開始 reformat 之前，建議先 confirm count unit、target export format、required fields 同 review owner，確保 output 一致同可用。」

docs.title: Sales Journey AI
docs.text: 這一節幫讀者把 AI idea 放回保險銷售 journey：user、pain point、data、human control、metric 和 compliance consideration。

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• 準備討論 AI 前，先讀 Sales Journey AI Notes。
  • 想知道「有啲咩位可以加 AI」，讀 AI Opportunities in Insurance。
  • 要準備 workshop / discovery，讀 Identification Playbook。
  • 會前想攞句子直接講，讀 Talking Points。
  • 如果要排序 use cases，跳到 AI Use Case Prioritisation。

docs.title: Key Reminder
docs.text: 先由低風險、agent-facing、approved-source、human-reviewed use cases 開始。Product recommendation、suitability assessment、underwriting decisioning 唔應該包裝成簡單 quick wins。

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Notes
docs.text: 這頁幫讀者用保險銷售 journey 角度理解 AI use cases，重點是 pain point、data、human control、metric 和 compliance。

docs.title: 概念
docs.text:
Sales Journey AI 是把 AI 放到保險銷售流程中，支援 agent、operations、underwriting 或 product team 做搜尋、摘要、檢查、解釋和下一步建議。

好的 AI use case 不應由技術開始，而應由 pain point 開始：

Pain point → user → data → AI support → human control → metric

docs.title: 為什麼對保險轉型重要
docs.text: 保險銷售涉及 suitability、披露、客戶資料、健康/財務資訊和 underwriting。AI 可以減少 manual work，但必須有 review、audit trail、approved sources 和清楚責任邊界。

docs.title: 簡單例子
docs.text: Journey Step Practical AI Support Control Product query Agent asks approved product/process questions Approved sources + citations Illustration Explain quote outputs in plain language Approved wording + disclaimer Application Check missing or inconsistent fields Agent confirms before submission Document upload Identify missing KYC/supporting documents Confidence threshold + exception queue BRD reformat Extract product rules from old templates Source traceability + reviewer sign-off

docs.title: Key Terms / 關鍵詞
docs.text: 專業詞 中文 / 說明 Grounding AI 回答基於已批准資料，不是自由發揮。 Human-in-the-loop 人負責確認重要輸出或決策。 Audit trail 保留輸出、來源、使用者、時間等記錄。 Data readiness 資料是否可用、結構化、準確、被批准使用。 Suitability guardrail 防止 AI 越過合規和銷售建議控制。

docs.title: BA / PM Relevance
docs.text: Lens 讀者要做什麼 BA 把 AI idea 連到 journey step、pain point、data source、control。 PM 定義 pilot scope、owner、approval path、success metric。 FDE 判斷是否需要 POS integration、logging、data pipeline 或 prototype。

docs.title: 讀者可以問
docs.text:
• Which journey pain point would this AI use case solve?
  • What data would the AI use, and is it approved?
  • Who reviews the output before it affects customer or case handling?
  • What metric would prove value: NIGO rate, cycle time, search time, rework?
  • Does this need to be inside current POS / sales platform, or can it start as a side assistant?

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: 「我建議每個 AI idea 都先用 user pain point、data readiness、human review point、compliance risk 同 measurable outcome 去評估，再決定 priority。」

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Identification Playbook
docs.text: 這頁係識別 Sales Journey AI opportunities 嘅 practical playbook，適合準備 Client POS / Sales Platform discovery 或 AI use case workshop。

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: 幫讀者系統化識別 Client POS / Sales Platform sales journey 入面可以加 AI 嘅位置，並分清楚低風險 quick wins 同需要 governance / compliance sign-off 嘅敏感 use cases。

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text:
• Team 問「sales journey 有咩 AI elements」。
  • 準備 AI use case workshop 或 discovery。
  • 要將 POS pain points 轉成 AI opportunity list。
  • 要避免將 product recommendation / underwriting decisioning 講成簡單 quick win。

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. Map journey
  2. Agent preparation、FNA、product search、illustration、application、document upload、submission、underwriting handoff、follow-up。
  3. Identify pain points
  4. 搵資料慢、文件錯漏、資料重複輸入、quote 難解釋、NIGO、case status 唔清楚。
  5. Classify user
  6. Agent、sales support、operations、underwriter、product/BA team、customer。
  7. Check source data
  8. Approved product guide、SOP、FAQ、BRD、application fields、document checklist、case status。
  9. Define AI support
  10. Search、summarize、classify documents、check completeness、explain quote、extract rules。
  11. Set controls
  12. Human review、approved source materials、citations、audit trail、role-based access、compliance sign-off。
  13. Split priority
  14. Low-risk quick win、controlled pilot、deep integration、sensitive/defer。

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text:
• 「邊個 journey step 最多 manual work 或 rework？」
  • 「Agent 最常要搵咩產品規則或流程資料？」
  • 「Top NIGO reasons 係咪可以用 pre-submission check 減少？」
  • 「有冇 approved product / process materials 可以俾 AI 做 grounded answer？」
  • 「AI output 係俾 agent 用、operations 用，定會直接俾 customer 睇？」
  • 「邊啲 use cases 需要 compliance / risk / legal sign-off？」
  • 「POS data 可唔可以 extract？定要先由 side assistant 開始？」

docs.title: Risk / Compliance Considerations
docs.text:
• Agent-facing AI 比 customer-facing AI 較易開始，但仍要 source control 和 logging。
  • Product recommendation 可能涉及 suitability，唔應該當 low-risk quick win。
  • Underwriting approval / rejection 唔應該由 AI 自動做，除非 governance 非常清楚。
  • Customer、health、financial data 使用前要確認 privacy、consent、retention。
  • Quote explanation 要用 approved wording，避免生成未經批准嘅 product claim。

docs.title: Expected Outputs
docs.text:
• Sales journey AI opportunity map。
  • Low-risk quick win shortlist。
  • Sensitive use case watchlist。
  • Data / POS dependency assumptions。
  • Governance and compliance questions log。
  • Recommended next step for each use case。

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Close
docs.text: 「我建議我哋先將 AI opportunities 分成 quick wins 同 sensitive use cases。Quick wins 可以由 agent-facing、approved-source、human-reviewed 場景開始；recommendation 或 underwriting 類就要先過 suitability、privacy、audit trail 同 compliance sign-off。」

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Opportunities in Insurance
docs.text: 這頁整理保險 sales journey 入面可以加 AI 嘅實際位置，並清楚分開低風險 quick wins 同敏感 use cases。

docs.title: 概念
docs.text:
Sales Journey AI Opportunities 係指喺保險銷售流程入面，搵出 AI 可以實際幫 agent、sales support、operations、underwriting 或 product team 減少錯漏、加快處理、提升解釋能力嘅位置。

重點唔係「AI 可以做咩」，而係：

邊個 user → 邊個 journey step → 咩 pain point → 用咩 approved data → 人點樣 review → 點樣量度成效

docs.title: 為什麼對 Client POS / Sales Platform 準備重要
docs.text:
current POS / sales platform 可能係 sales journey 入面嘅主要 front-office platform。AI 如果要落地，通常要同 current POS / sales platform journey、product rules、documents、case status、compliance controls 有關。

所以讀者應該先由低風險、agent-facing、可 review 嘅 use cases 開始講，避免一開始就將 AI product recommendation 或 underwriting decisioning 當 quick win。

docs.title: 低風險 Quick Wins
docs.text: Use Case User AI 幫咩 需要咩資料 Control Agent Knowledge Assistant Agent 查產品規則、流程、FAQ Approved product guides、SOP、FAQ Source citation、access control、usage log Document Checklist Validator Agent / operations 檢查文件是否齊全 Document checklist、uploaded documents Confidence threshold、exception queue Quote Explanation Assistant Agent 用易明語言解釋 illustration Illustration output、approved wording Approved template、disclaimer、agent review Pre-submission Quality Check Agent / sales support Submit 前檢查漏欄位和矛盾 Application fields、product rules、document status User confirmation、audit trail BRD Rule Extraction Product / BA team 從舊 template 抽 product rules Old BRDs、templates Source traceability、human sign-off

docs.title: 敏感 Use Cases
docs.text: Sensitive Area 為什麼敏感 要先確認 Product recommendation 可能影響 suitability 和 regulated advice Suitability guardrails、agent review、approval Suitability assessment 涉及客戶需要和銷售合規 Compliance sign-off、audit trail Underwriting decisioning 涉及風險接受、拒保、加費 Underwriting governance、human decision owner Customer-facing advice 可能被視為銷售建議 Approved wording、disclaimer、review process Health / financial data use 涉及敏感個人資料 Data access、retention、consent、privacy review

docs.title: BA / PM Relevance
docs.text: Lens 讀者要留意 BA 將 use case 連到 journey step、pain point、data source、business rule、control。 PM 確認 owner、priority、pilot scope、timeline、decision gate、risk approval。 FDE 判斷 data availability、POS integration、logging、prototype feasibility。

docs.title: 讀者可以問
docs.text:
• 呢個 AI use case 係解決邊個 journey pain point？
  • 係 agent-facing、operations-facing、underwriting-facing，定 customer-facing？
  • AI 會用咩資料？資料係咪 approved source？
  • Output 會由邊個 review？有冇 audit trail？
  • 成效用咩 metric 量度？NIGO rate、cycle time、search time、rework？
  • 呢個 use case 需要 embedded in current POS / sales platform，定可以先做 side assistant？

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: 「我會建議先由 lower-risk, agent-facing AI use cases 開始，例如 knowledge assistant、document checklist、quote explanation 同 pre-submission quality check。Product recommendation 或 underwriting support 就要再睇 suitability、data privacy、audit trail 同 human review controls。」

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Talking Points
docs.text: 這頁係 Sales Journey AI 討論用嘅 meeting-ready talking points，語氣貼近香港 team 對話。

docs.title: 一句定位
docs.text: 「Sales Journey AI 唔係一開始就做自動推薦或自動 underwriting，而係先搵啲低風險、agent-facing、可以 human review 嘅位置，幫 team 減少搵資料、文件錯漏、submission rework 同 quote explanation effort。」

docs.title: Quick Wins 可以咁講
docs.text:
• 「Agent Knowledge Assistant 可以先用 approved product guides、SOP、FAQ 做 grounded answer，幫 agent 快啲搵 product rules。」
  • 「Document Checklist Validator 可以幫 agent / operations 喺 submit 前檢查 KYC、medical form、income proof 等文件齊唔齊。」
  • 「Quote Explanation Assistant 可以用 approved wording 幫 agent 解釋 premium、benefits、exclusions 同 payment mode。」
  • 「Pre-submission Quality Check 可以 submit 前檢查 missing fields、資料矛盾、文件未齊，目標係減少 NIGO / rework。」
  • 「BRD Rule Extraction 可以幫 product / BA team 從 old templates 抽 eligibility、premium mode、rider rules，但一定要 human sign-off。」

docs.title: Sensitive Use Cases 要咁講
docs.text:
• 「Product recommendation 涉及 suitability，唔應該當普通 quick win。」
  • 「Underwriting approve / reject 涉及 risk decision，AI 最多先做 summary 或 triage support，decision owner 要清楚。」
  • 「Customer-facing advice 要小心 approved wording、disclaimer 同 compliance review。」
  • 「如果用到 health / financial data，要先確認 access、consent、retention 同 audit trail。」

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text:
• 「呢個 AI idea 係解決邊個 journey pain point？」
  • 「Output 係俾 agent 用，定會直接俾 customer 睇？」
  • 「AI answer 會 grounded 喺邊啲 approved source materials？」
  • 「邊個人 review AI output？有冇 audit trail？」
  • 「成功指標係 NIGO rate、cycle time、search time、rework，定 customer understanding？」
  • 「呢個 use case 需要 POS integration，定可以先做 side assistant？」

docs.title: Strong Close
docs.text: 「我會建議先用 value、data readiness、human review、compliance risk 同 POS dependency 去 prioritise，而唔係單純睇個 AI idea 聽落夠唔夠新。」

docs.title: Insurance AI Learning Library
docs.text:
This is a shareable learning and discussion library for insurance POS, sales journey AI, BRD/template reformatting, and AI use case prioritisation.

Disclaimer

This site is a personal learning and discussion library. It is not client advice, legal advice, compliance advice, or an official project recommendation.

docs.title: How To Use This Site
docs.text:
• Start with Client POS / Sales Platform Preparation and Insurance POS Basics to build context.
  • Use Sales Journey AI and AI Use Case Prioritisation for AI discussions.
  • Use Meeting / Discussion Outputs for ready-to-use questions and talking points.

docs.title: Working Principle
docs.text: Use an AI Transformation Pathfinder lens: connect business needs, delivery rhythm, system feasibility, data constraints, and AI governance.

docs.title: Start Here

docs.text:
Recommended pages

Focus

“To make the discussion actionable, I suggest capturing each pain point by journey step, owner, evidence, system touchpoint, control impact, and next action.”

docs.title: AI Use Case Prioritisation
docs.text: Prioritise AI use cases by business value, data readiness, risk, control requirements, user adoption, and integration dependency.

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• Use Prioritisation Playbook to define the method.
  • Use Use Case Matrix to compare users, pain points, data, controls, metrics, and dependencies.

docs.title: AI Use Case Prioritisation Playbook
docs.text: A prioritisation playbook for turning AI ideas into a shortlist based on pain point, value, data readiness, controls, and delivery dependency.

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. Start from pain point: NIGO, missing documents, quote explanation, product rules search.
  2. Define user and journey step: agent, sales support, operations, underwriter, product team.
  3. Confirm data source: product guide, SOP, application data, document checklist, BRD.
  4. Define AI support: search, summary, extraction, quality check, plain-language explanation.
  5. Design controls: citations, human review, audit log, approved wording, exception handling.
  6. Define metric: NIGO rate, cycle time, search time, manual rework, QA defects.
  7. Bucket: quick start, controlled pilot, deep integration, defer.

docs.title: Risk / Compliance Considerations
docs.text:
• Customer-facing language needs approval and version control.
  • Product recommendation support needs suitability guardrails.
  • Sensitive health/financial data needs access and retention controls.
  • AI output used in sales or underwriting workflow should be logged.
  • Automated decisioning should be treated as high risk unless governance is explicit.

  • Can it start outside the current POS / sales platform, or does it need integration?

docs.title: POS Assessment Playbook
docs.text: A practical playbook for assessing a current POS / sales platform across journey, users, systems, data, product rules, vendor dependencies, controls, and AI opportunities.

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Assess the current POS / sales platform current state and translate pain points into evidence-based options and next-step recommendations.

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text:
• Before a POS discovery workshop.
  • When preparing a current-state assessment approach.
  • When turning POS pain points into recommendations.

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. Confirm scope: product line, channel, users, system boundary, expected output.
  2. Map current-state journey: needs analysis, illustration, application, documents, submission, underwriting handoff.
  3. Collect evidence: process maps, screenshots, NIGO reasons, case logs, sample BRDs, user feedback.
  4. Identify pain point and root cause: process, system, data, vendor, or control issue.
  5. Assess POS capabilities: quote, validation, document checklist, case tracking, reporting, configuration.
  6. Shape options: optimise process, enhance platform, add AI assistant, improve integration, vendor change, longer-term platform change.

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text: Topic Questions Scope Which products, channels, and user groups are in scope? Journey Which steps are inside the current POS / sales platform and which are outside? Pain point Where do agents or operations teams spend the most time? Data Which data is structured, reused, or rekeyed? Product rules Which rules are configured, manual, or vendor-controlled? Metrics Do we have NIGO rate, STP rate, cycle time, drop-off, or rework volume?

docs.title: Risk / Compliance Considerations
docs.text:
• Suitability and disclosure steps must be traceable.
  • Customer, health, and financial data require access and retention controls.
  • AI outputs should not bypass underwriting, compliance, or adviser review.
  • Pain points should be supported by evidence, not only anecdotes.

docs.title: Expected Outputs
docs.text:
• Current-state journey map with system touchpoints.
  • Pain point and root cause summary.
  • POS capability gap table.
  • Vendor and dependency assessment.
  • AI opportunity list with governance notes.

docs.title: Insurance POS Basics
docs.text: Learn the core insurance POS concepts needed to discuss sales journeys, product rules, validation, NIGO, and system touchpoints.

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• Read POS Basics if you are new to insurance POS.
  • Read Product Configuration when discussing product rules, configuration, BRD, or export fields.

docs.title: Insurance POS Basics
docs.text: Insurance POS is the front-office sales platform that turns a customer conversation into needs analysis, quote/illustration, application capture, document submission, and case tracking.

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: POS is the entry point of the insurance sales journey. Poor POS design can create duplicate data entry, quote explanation issues, missing documents, NIGO, manual referrals, slow product launches, and weaker customer experience.

docs.title: Typical Journey
docs.text: Step Example What To Observe Needs analysis Capture protection, savings, health needs Is the process guided and auditable? Product selection Select base plan and riders Are rules system-driven or manually interpreted? Illustration Generate premium / benefits projection Is it easy to explain? Application Capture proposal / e-application data Is data reused or rekeyed? Document upload Upload KYC, medical, financial documents Are documents checked before submission? Submission Send to underwriting / operations What causes NIGO or referral?

docs.title: Key Terms
docs.text: Term Meaning FNA / needs analysis Customer needs analysis supporting suitability and recommendation rationale. Illustration Premium, benefit, cash value, or projection output. Rider Optional add-on benefit. Suitability Whether the product fits the customer's needs and circumstances. NIGO Not in good order; application is incomplete or incorrect. STP Straight-through processing without manual rework. Referral Manual review due to rules, risk, or exceptions.

docs.title: BA / PM Relevance
docs.text: Lens What To Do BA Map journey, fields, rules, pain points, and controls. PM Clarify scope, stakeholders, timeline, outputs, and dependencies. FDE Understand where data is created, how it moves, and what can be automated.

docs.title: Questions To Ask
docs.text:
• Which steps are performed inside the current POS / sales platform?
  • Which steps happen outside it, such as email, Excel, CRM, or manual review?
  • What are the top reasons for NIGO or referral?
  • Which product rules are validated before submission?
  • What evidence do we have: screenshots, process map, case logs, user feedback?

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: “Can I confirm whether the current POS / sales platform scope covers quote, illustration, application capture, document upload, and submission, or only part of that journey?”

docs.title: Insurance Product Configuration Basics
docs.text: Product configuration translates product rules into system behaviour, including eligibility, riders, premium modes, validation rules, document requirements, and referral triggers.

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: Unclear product rules can cause quote errors, blocked applications, NIGO, manual referrals, and slow product launches or product changes.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Rule Type Example System Impact Eligibility Applicant age must be 18-60 POS validates age basis and error messages. Premium mode Monthly / annual payment available Impacts illustration, payment, and export fields. Rider rule Rider only available with selected base plans Impacts product selection and validation. Underwriting trigger Medical review needed above threshold Impacts referral and document checklist.

docs.title: Key Terms
docs.text: Term Meaning Base plan Main product. Rider Optional add-on product or benefit. Eligibility rule Who can buy and when. Validation rule System check against business rules. Referral rule When manual review is required. Configuration owner Team responsible for maintaining system rules.

docs.title: BA / PM Relevance
docs.text: Lens What To Watch BA Rule wording, exceptions, source traceability, business owner. PM Product change timeline, vendor dependency, approval path, testing window. FDE Rule location, data structure, API/import format, test cases.

docs.title: Questions To Ask
docs.text:
• Which rules are configured in the current POS / sales platform versus handled manually?
  • Who owns product rule changes: product, IT, vendor, underwriting, or compliance?
  • How are riders and eligibility rules tested before launch?
  • What happens if BRD wording conflicts with system behaviour?

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: “For BRD/template reformatting, I would separate documentation clean-up from rules that need to become POS configuration, validation logic, or export fields.”

docs.text: Stakeholder-based discovery questions for clarifying scope, pain points, system touchpoints, data readiness, and risk/control considerations.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:

docs.title: Business / Product Owner
docs.text:

docs.text:

docs.title: Product BRD / Template Reformat
docs.text: Prepare for BRD/template reformatting by clarifying count unit, target format, source traceability, product rule ownership, and review process.

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• Use the playbook to clarify what is included in the batch of legacy templates/items.
  • Pair this with Product Configuration to understand how rules become system behaviour.

docs.title: Product BRD Reformat Playbook
docs.text: A practical playbook for converting legacy product BRDs/templates into structured, reviewable, traceable, and export-ready requirements.

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Turn old product BRDs/templates into a consistent requirement structure that can support product governance, POS configuration, migration, reporting, or automation.

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text:
• When legacy templates need to be reformatted.
  • When the batch of legacy templates/items is not yet clearly defined.
  • When product documents need to become structured rules, trackers, or export fields.

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. Confirm count unit: product, rider, document, rule, section, export row.
  2. Build inventory: ID, source file, owner, format, status, open questions.
  3. Extract facts exactly as written before rewriting.
  4. Standardise requirement language: must, should, may, exception, open question.
  5. Map to POS / downstream impact: validation, screen field, document checklist, referral, export field.
  6. Review and sign off with product, system, compliance, or underwriting owners as needed.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Old Wording Standard Requirement Export / System Impact Open Question Applicant should be 18-60 Applicant must be aged 18 to 60 inclusive at application date min_age, max_age, age_basis Applies to rider also? Monthly payment available Product must support monthly premium mode where eligible payment_mode=monthly Any minimum premium? Medical may be needed POS must flag medical referral based on defined underwriting rules referral trigger Rule stored in POS or underwriting system?

docs.title: Expected Outputs
docs.text:
• BRD inventory tracker.
  • Standardised requirement template.
  • Open question log with owners.
  • Export field mapping.
  • Review / approval status.

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: “Before reformatting, we should confirm count unit, target export format, required fields, and review owner so the output is consistent and usable.”

docs.title: Sales Journey AI
docs.text: Explore practical AI opportunities across the insurance sales journey, with emphasis on agent-facing support, approved source materials, human review, audit trail, and compliance sign-off.

docs.title: How To Use This Page
docs.text:
• Read Sales Journey AI Notes before discussing AI.
  • Read AI Opportunities in Insurance to understand where AI can fit.
  • Use Identification Playbook for workshop or discovery preparation.
  • Use Talking Points before meetings.

docs.title: Key Reminder
docs.text: Start with lower-risk, agent-facing, approved-source, human-reviewed use cases. Do not present product recommendation, suitability assessment, or underwriting decisioning as simple quick wins.

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Notes
docs.text: AI in the sales journey should be framed around user pain points, approved data, human review, controls, and measurable outcomes.

docs.title: Concept
docs.text:
Sales Journey AI supports agents, operations, underwriters, or product teams with search, summarisation, checks, explanation, and next-action support.

Pain point → user → data → AI support → human control → metric

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: Insurance sales involves suitability, disclosures, customer data, health/financial information, and underwriting. AI can reduce manual work, but it needs review, audit trail, approved sources, and clear accountability.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Journey Step AI Support Control Product query Agent asks approved product/process questions Approved sources + citations Illustration Explain quote outputs in plain language Approved wording + disclaimer Application Check missing or inconsistent fields Agent confirms before submission Document upload Identify missing KYC/supporting documents Confidence threshold + exception queue BRD reformat Extract product rules from old templates Source traceability + reviewer sign-off

docs.title: Key Terms
docs.text: Term Meaning Grounding AI answers are based on approved sources. Human-in-the-loop A person reviews important outputs or decisions. Audit trail Logs of output, source, user, and time. Data readiness Data is available, structured, accurate, and approved. Suitability guardrail Control preventing AI from bypassing sales suitability requirements.

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: “I suggest assessing each AI idea by user pain point, data readiness, human review point, compliance risk, and measurable outcome before prioritising it.”

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Identification Playbook
docs.text: A practical playbook for identifying AI opportunities across the insurance sales journey while separating quick wins from sensitive use cases.

docs.title: Step-by-Step Approach
docs.text:
1. Map journey: agent preparation, FNA, product search, illustration, application, documents, submission, underwriting handoff, follow-up.
  2. Identify pain points: slow search, missing documents, duplicate entry, hard-to-explain quotes, NIGO, unclear status.
  3. Classify user: agent, sales support, operations, underwriter, product/BA team, customer.
  4. Check source data: product guide, SOP, FAQ, BRD, application fields, document checklist, case status.
  5. Define AI support: search, summarise, classify documents, check completeness, explain quote, extract rules.
  6. Set controls: human review, approved sources, citations, audit trail, role-based access, compliance sign-off.
  7. Split priority: quick win, controlled pilot, deep integration, sensitive/defer.

docs.title: Discovery Questions
docs.text:
• Which journey step has the most manual work or rework?
  • What product/process information do agents search for most often?
  • Can top NIGO reasons be reduced through pre-submission checks?
  • Are there approved product/process materials for grounded answers?
  • Which use cases need compliance / risk / legal sign-off?

docs.title: Risk / Compliance Considerations
docs.text:
• Agent-facing AI is easier to start than customer-facing AI, but still needs source control and logging.
  • Product recommendation may affect suitability and should not be treated as a low-risk quick win.
  • Underwriting approval/rejection should not be automated without explicit governance.
  • Customer, health, and financial data require privacy, consent, and retention review.

docs.title: Expected Outputs
docs.text:
• Sales journey AI opportunity map.
  • Low-risk quick win shortlist.
  • Sensitive use case watchlist.
  • Data / POS dependency assumptions.
  • Governance and compliance questions log.

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Close
docs.text: “Let’s separate quick wins from sensitive use cases. Quick wins should start with agent-facing, approved-source, human-reviewed workflows; recommendation and underwriting use cases need suitability, privacy, audit trail, and compliance sign-off.”

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Opportunities in Insurance
docs.text: Start with lower-risk, agent-facing AI use cases such as knowledge search, document checks, quote explanation, and pre-submission quality checks. Treat product recommendation and underwriting decisioning as sensitive use cases.

docs.title: Low-Risk Quick Wins
docs.text: Use Case User AI Support Data Needed Control Agent knowledge assistant Agent Search product rules, process, FAQ Approved product guides, SOP, FAQ Citations, access control, usage log Document checklist validator Agent / operations Check document completeness Checklist, uploaded documents Confidence threshold, exception queue Quote explanation assistant Agent Explain illustration in plain language Illustration output, approved wording Template, disclaimer, agent review Pre-submission quality check Agent / sales support Check missing fields and inconsistencies Application fields, product rules, document status User confirmation, audit trail BRD rule extraction Product / BA team Extract product rules from old templates Legacy BRDs/templates Source traceability, human sign-off

docs.title: Sensitive Use Cases
docs.text: Sensitive Area Why Sensitive Confirm First Product recommendation May affect suitability and regulated advice Suitability guardrails, adviser review, approval Suitability assessment Sales compliance and customer needs Compliance sign-off, audit trail Underwriting decisioning Risk acceptance, rejection, loading Underwriting governance, human decision owner Customer-facing advice May be treated as advice Approved wording, disclaimer, review process Health / financial data use Sensitive personal data Access, retention, consent, privacy review

docs.title: Questions To Ask
docs.text:
• Which journey pain point does this AI use case solve?
  • Is it agent-facing, operations-facing, underwriting-facing, or customer-facing?
  • What data does AI use, and is it an approved source?
  • Who reviews the output, and is there an audit trail?
  • What metric proves value: NIGO rate, cycle time, search time, or rework?

docs.title: Meeting-Ready Wording
docs.text: “I suggest starting with lower-risk, agent-facing AI use cases such as knowledge assistant, document checklist, quote explanation, and pre-submission quality check. Product recommendation and underwriting support need separate review for suitability, data privacy, audit trail, and human control.”

docs.title: Sales Journey AI Talking Points
docs.text: Use these lines for professional discussions about Sales Journey AI opportunities.

docs.title: Positioning
docs.text: “Sales Journey AI should not start with automated recommendation or automated underwriting. We should start with lower-risk, agent-facing, human-reviewed support that reduces search effort, document errors, submission rework, and quote explanation effort.”

docs.title: Quick Wins
docs.text:
• “Agent knowledge assistant can use approved product guides, SOPs, and FAQs for grounded answers.”
  • “Document checklist validation can help reduce missing documents and NIGO.”
  • “Quote explanation support should use approved wording and adviser review.”
  • “Pre-submission quality checks can reduce missing fields, inconsistent information, and rework.”
  • “BRD rule extraction can support product/BA teams, but output needs source traceability and human sign-off.”

docs.title: Sensitive Use Cases
docs.text:
• “Product recommendation affects suitability and should not be treated as a simple quick win.”
  • “Underwriting approve/reject decisions require clear human decision ownership.”
  • “Customer-facing advice needs approved wording, disclaimer, and compliance review.”
  • “Health and financial data require access, consent, retention, and audit controls.”

docs.title: Strong Close
docs.text: “I suggest prioritising by value, data readiness, human review, compliance risk, and POS dependency, rather than by how innovative the AI idea sounds.”
